skill.name: framer-motion
skill.description: Framer Motion reference and templates for building React and Next.js animations, including variants, gestures, page transitions, scroll effects, and animated lists.
skill.summary: Build polished React animations with Framer Motion patterns, references, and reusable templates.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported 361 potential issues, but review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, Framer Motion animation terminology, and ordinary React UI identifiers. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, network calls, filesystem access, or real external command execution was found. The skill is safe to publish as documentation and reusable React templates.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many triple-backtick Markdown fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed locations are documentation fences for bash installation commands and TypeScript examples, not executable skill logic or dynamic command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or comments around React usage. No shell execution API, dynamic command construction, or runtime command dispatch was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Animation Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged words such as fade, scale, and transition as weak cryptographic algorithm indicators. In context these are Framer Motion animation names and visual effects, not cryptographic primitives or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use animation vocabulary and CSS class names. They do not implement hashing, encryption, signing, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: React UI Identifiers Flagged as Secrets or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged React keys, keyboard event handlers, grid CSS classes, and item identifiers as certificate, key, or reconnaissance patterns. These are ordinary UI code examples with local component state only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines manipulate local React state and DOM event handlers. No certificate material, private keys, host enumeration, network calls, or filesystem inspection were found.

content.user_title: Build React Animations with Framer Motion
content.value_statement: React teams often need polished motion without rebuilding animation patterns from scratch. This skill provides Framer Motion references, examples, and templates for common UI animation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains core Framer Motion concepts such as motion components, variants, gestures, and hooks.
Provides reusable TypeScript templates for page transitions and animated lists.
Shows examples for hover, tap, drag, reorder, layout, and exit animations.
Includes scroll-triggered and scroll-linked animation patterns for React interfaces.
Demonstrates reduced-motion handling for more accessible animation behavior.
Supports React and Next.js workflows through documented examples.
limitations: It does not install dependencies or run project commands for the user.
It assumes the project already uses React or Next.js.
It does not provide visual previews of the animations.
Examples may need style and component adjustments for a specific design system.

use_cases.title: Add Product UI Animations
use_cases.description: Use documented variants and gesture examples to add hover states, transitions, and animated component entrances.

use_cases.title: Build Next.js Page Transitions
use_cases.description: Use the page transition template to wrap routes and create consistent enter and exit animations.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Patterns
use_cases.description: Request implementation-ready examples for lists, cards, scroll reveals, and micro-interactions during design exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Fade Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the framer-motion skill to add a simple fade and slide entrance animation to my React component. Keep the animation subtle and accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a Dynamic List
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate list entry and exit states
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the framer-motion skill to build an animated list with staggered entrance, smooth removal, and layout transitions for changing items.

prompt_templates.title: Add Next.js Page Transitions
prompt_templates.scenario: Route animation in a Next.js app
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the framer-motion skill to implement page transitions for a Next.js App Router project. Include reduced-motion handling and explain where each component belongs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Motion System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the framer-motion skill to define reusable variants, gesture states, page transitions, and scroll animations for a production React design system.

output_examples.input: Animate a group of cards as they enter the viewport.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with staggered container variants, card item variants, viewport settings, and reduced-motion adjustments.

output_examples.input: Create page transitions for a Next.js dashboard.
output_examples.output: A route transition approach using a provider component, selected transition variants, placement guidance, and notes about App Router behavior.
Recommended hover and tap motion values, timing guidance, accessibility notes, and integration steps for the existing button component.

best_practices: Keep motion subtle and tied to user intent, navigation, or state changes.
Use variants for shared animation states instead of repeating values across components.
Respect reduced-motion preferences for entrance, scroll, and route animations.
anti_patterns: Do not animate every element on a page without a user experience reason.
Do not use long or bouncy transitions for frequent workflow actions.
Do not ignore layout shifts, focus behavior, or reduced-motion preferences.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Framer Motion animations for React and Next.js interfaces.

faq.question: Does it include reusable components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes TypeScript templates for page transitions and animated lists.

faq.question: Can it help with scroll animations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes scroll-triggered reveals and scroll-linked animation examples.

faq.question: Does it run commands or modify my project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates. The user or agent applies changes in the target project.

faq.question: Is it suitable for accessibility-aware animation?
faq.answer: Yes. The references include reduced-motion guidance and patterns for avoiding unnecessary movement.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
